data.title: Valaris Ltd SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Valaris Ltd, a global offshore contract drilling company, has released its 2025 10-K report, detailing significant financial and operational achievements, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the offshore drilling industry. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's performance, strategic direction, and risk management strategies.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Operating Revenues: $2,369.0 million, remained relatively flat compared to 2024, driven by a net decrease from fewer operating days offset by higher average daily revenues.
  • Operating Income: $477.0 million, increased by 35% from 2024, primarily due to lower contract drilling expenses and higher equity in earnings of ARO.
  • Net Income: $979.1 million, increased by 165% from 2024, largely due to a significant income tax benefit and higher operating income.
  • Net Income Attributable to Valaris: $982.8 million, reflecting a substantial increase from the previous year, driven by improved operational efficiencies and tax benefits.
  • Basic Earnings Per Share: $13.92, up from $5.18 in 2024, reflecting the significant increase in net income.
  • Diluted EPS: $13.86, increased from $5.12 in 2024, indicating strong financial performance and profitability.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Valaris Limited operates a fleet of 46 rigs, including 13 drillships, two semisubmersible rigs, and 31 jackup rigs. The company provides offshore drilling services to leading international and government-owned oil and gas companies across six continents.
  • Pending Business Combination: Valaris has entered into a Business Combination Agreement with Transocean Ltd., under which Transocean will acquire all issued and outstanding common shares of Valaris. This combination is expected to result in Transocean's existing shareholders owning approximately 53% and Valaris' existing shareholders owning approximately 47% of the combined company.
  • Industry Dynamics: The offshore drilling industry is cyclical, influenced by global energy demand and oil and gas supply dynamics. Despite current oversupply, the market is expected to shift to a tighter supply-demand balance, driven by underinvestment in upstream development and slowing production growth from non-OPEC sources.
  • Contract Drilling Operations: Valaris operates through four segments: Floaters, Jackups, ARO, and Other. The company provides drilling services on a day rate contract basis, with customers bearing most costs and economic risks related to well success.
  • Geographical Performance: Valaris operates in diverse regions, including the Gulf of America, South America, the North Sea, the Mediterranean, the Middle East, Africa, and Asia Pacific. Non-U.S. operations accounted for 86% of total consolidated revenues in 2025.
  • Fleet Utilization and Performance: As of December 31, 2025, Valaris had 46 rigs, with 35 active. The company experienced a decrease in floater utilization to 54% and an increase in jackup utilization to 61%. ARO's utilization improved to 86%.
  • Revenue Segments: In 2025, floater revenues decreased by 11% due to fewer operating days, while jackup revenues increased by 20% due to higher average daily revenues and more operating days. ARO revenues increased by 11%, driven by new contracts and higher day rates.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates improved market conditions for floaters as rigs commence new contracts in 2026. Jackup utilization remains strong, supported by national oil companies' focus on energy security and infrastructure development.
  • Operational Challenges: The company faces challenges such as rig reactivation costs, competitive bidding for contracts, and geopolitical risks. Inflationary pressures and currency fluctuations also impact operational costs.
  • Sustainability Initiatives: Valaris is focused on sustainability, with a dedicated department and board committee overseeing policies and practices. The company emphasizes responsible energy solutions and stakeholder engagement.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Business Combination with Transocean: Valaris entered into a Business Combination Agreement with Transocean, under which Transocean will acquire all issued and outstanding common shares of Valaris. This strategic move is expected to enhance the company's market position and operational capabilities.
  • Focus on High-Return Assets: Valaris is focusing on ultra-deepwater floater and premium jackup operations, while de-emphasizing other assets that do not meet economic return standards.
  • Joint Venture with Saudi Aramco: The company is involved in a joint venture with Saudi Aramco, ARO, which is expected to contribute to future growth through its newbuild jackup rig program.
  • Capital Management: Valaris issued $1.1 billion in 8.375% Senior Secured Second Lien Notes due 2030 to strengthen its capital structure. The company has a $375 million senior secured revolving credit agreement maturing in 2028, with no debt principal payments due until 2030. Valaris repurchased $100 million of its shares in 2025 under an authorized $600 million share repurchase program, with $175 million remaining available for future repurchases. The company also sold several rigs, generating $136 million in net proceeds, to optimize its asset portfolio.
  • Future Outlook: Valaris plans to fund its short-term liquidity needs from cash and cash equivalents, cash flows from operations, and available credit facilities. The company expects capital expenditures in 2026 to range between $425 million and $475 million, primarily for maintenance and upgrades. Valaris aims to continue its focus on high-return assets and may pursue additional capital expenditures for rig upgrades and acquisitions, subject to market conditions and restrictions within the Business Combination Agreement. The company is also exploring opportunities to monetize non-core assets to enhance shareholder value and improve liquidity.

Challenges and Risks

  • Business Combination Risks: The pending Business Combination between Valaris and Transocean presents significant risks, including potential delays or failure to complete the transaction, which could adversely affect the market price of common shares and result in substantial termination fees. The combination is subject to various closing conditions and regulatory approvals, and failure to meet these could lead to termination of the agreement. Additionally, the agreement limits Valaris's ability to pursue alternative transactions, potentially discouraging competing offers.
  • Operational Risks: Dependency on offshore oil and natural gas exploration, which is highly sensitive to volatile commodity prices. The industry is cyclical, and prolonged periods of reduced demand or excess rig supply could necessitate idling or scrapping rigs, adversely affecting financial performance. The company also faces challenges in securing contracts on favorable terms, with potential early terminations or renegotiations by customers impacting revenue.
  • Cybersecurity Threats: Cybersecurity threats pose a significant risk, with potential disruptions to operations and financial systems. Despite having a cybersecurity program, the evolving threat landscape and the use of AI introduce new vulnerabilities. The company may incur substantial costs in response to incidents, which could materially affect financial results.
  • ARO Joint Venture Risks: The ARO joint venture with Saudi Aramco involves risks related to control over operations and financial matters, with potential impacts from customer concentration and financing obligations for newbuild jackup rigs. The joint venture's reliance on Saudi Aramco and potential funding requirements could strain liquidity.
  • Regulatory Risks: Compliance with anti-corruption laws like the FCPA and UKBA, with violations potentially leading to substantial fines and operational restrictions. The company also faces increasing regulatory complexity in offshore drilling, with potential for significant capital expenditures to comply with new laws.
  • Sustainability and Climate Change: Sustainability initiatives and climate change regulations could impact demand for offshore drilling services. Global efforts to reduce GHG emissions may lead to reduced demand for petroleum products, affecting the company's operations and profitability.
  • Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by the cyclical nature of the offshore drilling industry and the need to adapt to changing market conditions. Strategies include focusing on cost management, maintaining operational efficiency, and exploring opportunities for strategic partnerships or acquisitions to enhance competitiveness.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in oil and natural gas prices, which directly affect drilling activity levels. Foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations also pose a risk, potentially impacting financial results due to the global nature of operations. The company employs hedging strategies to mitigate some of these risks, but market volatility remains a significant concern.

SEC Filing: Valaris Ltd [ VAL ] - 10-K - Feb. 20, 2026

data.title: ATI INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
ATI Inc., a leading producer of high-performance materials and components, has released its annual 10-K report, showcasing a robust financial performance driven by strong demand in the aerospace and defense markets. The report highlights significant growth in key financial metrics, strategic investments, and a clear focus on future growth opportunities, while also addressing the challenges and risks faced by the company.

Financial Highlights

  • Sales: $4,587.4 million, reflecting a 5% increase from fiscal year 2024, driven by strong demand in the aerospace & defense markets.
  • Gross Profit: $1,007.0 million, representing 22.0% of sales, an improvement of 140 basis points from fiscal year 2024 due to favorable sales mix and pricing.
  • Operating Income: $640.9 million, a 5% increase year-over-year, supported by growth in aerospace & defense sales.
  • Net Income: $404.3 million, a 10% increase from fiscal year 2024, attributed to higher sales and improved operational efficiencies.
  • Diluted Net Income Per Share: $2.85, up from $2.55 in fiscal year 2024, reflecting the company's strong financial performance and share repurchase activities.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: ATI operates in two main business segments: High Performance Materials & Components (HPMC) and Advanced Alloys & Solutions (AA&S). HPMC focuses on aerospace & defense markets, contributing 92% of its revenue, with significant sales in commercial jet engines. AA&S serves aerospace & defense, specialty energy, and other industrial markets, with aerospace & defense comprising 41% of its total revenue.
  • Geographical Performance: International sales accounted for 43% of total sales, with significant contributions from Europe and Asia. The U.S. remains the largest market, representing 57% of total sales.
  • Sales Units: The HPMC segment saw a 7% increase in sales, driven by a 14% rise in aerospace & defense markets. The AA&S segment experienced a 3% sales increase, with a notable 15% growth in aerospace & defense sales.
  • New Product Launches: ATI continues to develop advanced metallic powders for next-generation aerospace products, including additive manufacturing applications, to meet increasing demand in the aerospace & defense sectors.
  • New Production Launches: ATI is expanding its production capacity for advanced metallic powders and has a dedicated additive manufacturing facility to cater to the aerospace & defense demand.
  • Future Outlook: ATI anticipates continued growth in the aerospace & defense markets, driven by long-term supply agreements and increasing demand for next-generation jet engines and airframes. The company is well-positioned to capitalize on the expected multi-year demand growth in these sectors.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Investments: ATI Inc. focused on enhancing its operational efficiency and market position by investing $280.6 million in capital expenditures, primarily targeting growth in the aerospace and defense market. The company also amended its Asset Based Lending (ABL) Credit Facility to extend its term through June 2030, providing additional financial flexibility and liquidity.
  • Capital Management: ATI Inc. actively managed its capital structure by repurchasing approximately 6.4 million shares of its common stock for $470 million under its Share Repurchase Program, with $120 million of authorization remaining. The company also reduced its debt by repaying $150 million of debentures and redeemed $291.4 million of 3.5% Convertible Senior Notes, which contributed to a decrease in interest expense. Additionally, ATI maintained a strong liquidity position with $1.1 billion in total liquidity, including $417 million in cash and cash equivalents.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead, ATI plans to continue its disciplined approach to capital allocation, focusing on reducing debt and enhancing shareholder value through share repurchases. The company aims to leverage its improved financial position to explore strategic growth opportunities, particularly in the aerospace and defense sectors, while maintaining a strong balance sheet to support future investments and operational needs.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Cyclicality: ATI Inc. operates in highly cyclical industries, particularly aerospace and defense, which represent a significant portion of its sales. The company is exposed to fluctuations in demand due to economic conditions and geopolitical factors.
  • Supply Chain Risks: ATI relies on a global supply chain for critical raw materials, which are subject to price volatility and availability risks, particularly from politically unstable regions.
  • Operational Risks: The company faces risks related to manufacturing disruptions, cybersecurity threats, and the recruitment and retention of skilled labor.
  • Geopolitical Risks: ATI is exposed to risks from geopolitical events, such as the ongoing conflict between Russia and Ukraine, which could disrupt supply chains and affect material prices.
  • Market Risks: ATI is exposed to market risks related to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, which could impact its financial results. The company also faces risks from changes in interest rates, particularly given its substantial level of indebtedness.
  • Management Strategies: Management is focused on operational improvements to manage working capital and reduce inventory intensity. ATI is also actively managing its capital allocation strategy, including share repurchases and debt reduction, to strengthen its financial position.

SEC Filing: ATI INC [ ATI ] - 10-K - Feb. 20, 2026

data.title: Dollar Steady After Trump's Remarks
data.description:
The US Dollar Index held mostly flat near 97.8 on Friday, recovering from an initial dip after the Supreme Court struck down President Trump's reciprocal tariffs.

While the ruling initially weighed on the greenback by removing an inflationary tailwind, the dollar found support after Trump countered by vowing to sign an executive order for a new 10% global tariff.

This swift pivot back to protectionism offset the downbeat Q4 GDP print of 1.4%, which showed the economic toll of the government shutdown and trade duties.

The dollar's downside was limited by sticky December core PCE inflation of 3%, reinforcing expectations that the Fed will maintain a restrictive stance.

Despite the legal setback for the White House, the prospect of new broad-based levies and persistent price pressures helped the index maintain its footing.

The dollar remains on track for a weekly gain as markets balance the potential for tariff refunds against the administration's commitment to stronger trade barriers.

data.title: SEC probe involving AppLovin still active, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission's investigation into marketing platform AppLovin is continuing, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.

Bloomberg had in October reported that the SEC was probing allegations that AppLovin violated platform partners' service agreements to push more targeted advertising to consumers.

The SEC declined to release documents related to the AppLovin probe when requested by Bloomberg. When appealed, the agency said that an "investigation involving AppLovin is still active and ongoing."

It did not share correspondence between staffers referencing AppLovin as it might "cause harm to the ongoing and active enforcement investigation," the SEC said in a letter to Bloomberg on Friday.

Individuals and entities relevant to the investigation could "fabricate evidence, influence witness testimony and/or destroy or alter certain documents," according to the report.

The correspondence might also reveal cooperating witnesses, the SEC said in its letter to Bloomberg. It has not specified the subject or scope of the investigation nor has it accused AppLovin or its officials of wrongdoing.

Reuters could not independently verify the report. The U.S. SEC and AppLovin did not immediately respond to Reuters' requests for comment.

The investigation into AppLovin is reportedly in response to a whistleblower complaint filed last year, along with multiple short-seller reports.

data.title: ICE Review: Canola Up Despite U.S. Tariff Uncertainty
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--The ICE Futures canola market recovered from early losses to settle with small gains in the most active months as traders digested the latest tariff uncertainty out of the United States.

* The U.S. Supreme Court ruled 6-3 Friday that President Donald Trump's broad global tariffs were illegal, sparking sharp losses in soybeans and soyoil amid uncertainty over what the ruling will mean for trade. Trump later expressed disappointment in the decision but said he would now impose a new 10 per cent global tariff under different legislation "over and above our normal tariffs already being charged." * Canola initially fell in sympathy with soybeans and soyoil but eventually found some independent strength as the session progressed.

* The May contract dipped below chart support at C$680 per tonne. The nearby technical signals remain pointing higher, and the early losses encouraged some buying interest that eventually took prices to new six-month highs.

* Canada exported 257,100 tonnes of canola during the week ended Feb. 15, which was up 38 per cent from the previous week, reported the Canadian Grain Commission. Crop-year-to- date canola exports of just over four million tonnes compare with 5.8 million at the same point in 2024/25.

* There were 74,948 contracts traded on Friday, which compares with Thursday when 84,036 contracts changed hands.

Spreading was a feature, accounting for 51,256 of the contracts traded.

Settlement prices in Canadian dollars per metric tonne.

data.title: AGL Class Action Reminder: Stockholders Who Lost Money Investing in Agilon Health, Inc. Should Contact Robbins LLP for Information About Recovering Their Losses
data.description:
SAN DIEGO, CA / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2026 / Robbins LLP reminds stockholders that a class action was filed on behalf of all investors who purchased or otherwise acquired agilon health, inc. securities between February 26, 2025 and August 4, 2025. Agilon describes itself as the "trusted partner empowering physicians to transform health care in our communities."

For more information, submit a form, email attorney Aaron Dumas, Jr., or give us a call at (800) 350-6003.

What are the allegations? Robbins LLP is Investigating Allegations that Agilon Health, Inc. (AGL) Misled Investors Regarding its Business Prospects

According to the complaint, defendants failed to disclose that they recklessly issued guidance for 2025 that they knew or should have known was not going to be achieved, given material industry headwinds of which they were aware, and materially overstated the immediate positive financial impact from "strategic actions" taken by agilon to reduce risk.

On August 4, 2025, agilon issued a press release announcing that Steven Sell had stepped down as President, CEO and a Director of the Board. Plaintiff alleges that the Company's form 8-K filed with the SEC stated that "Mr. Sell's departure was a termination without 'cause' under Mr. Sell's employment agreement[.]" The complaint further alleges that on August 4, 2025, agilon issued disappointing financial results. On this news, the price of the Company's stock fell over 50%, to close at $0.8801 on August 5, 2025.

What can you do now? You may be eligible to participate in the class action against agilon health, inc. Shareholders who wish to serve as lead plaintiff for the class must submit their papers to the court by March 2, 2026. The lead plaintiff is a representative party who acts on behalf of other class members in directing the litigation. You do not have to participate in the case to be eligible for a recovery. If you choose to take no action, you can remain an absent class member. For more information, click here.

All representation is on a contingency fee basis. Shareholders pay no fees or expenses.

About Robbins LLP: A recognized leader in shareholder rights litigation, the attorneys and staff of Robbins LLP have been dedicated to helping shareholders recover losses, improve corporate governance structures, and hold company executives accountable for their wrongdoing since 2002.

To be notified if a class action against agilon health, inc. settles or to receive free alerts when corporate executives engage in wrongdoing, sign up for Stock Watch today.

Attorney Advertising. Past results do not guarantee a similar outcome.

San Diego, CA 92122

data.title: $CRWV Stock Drop Alert: CoreWeave, Inc. Stock Drops an Additional 12% Prior to Earnings after Analysts Commentary on Execution Risks - Investors Reminded of the Class Action
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2026 / Leading securities law firm Bleichmar Fonti & Auld LLP announces that a class action lawsuit has been filed against CoreWeave, Inc. and certain of the Company's senior executives for securities fraud after significant stock drops resulting from the potential violations of the federal securities laws.

If you invested in CoreWeave, you are encouraged to obtain additional information by visiting: https://www.bfalaw.com/cases/coreweave-inc-class-action-lawsuit.

Investors have until March 13, 2026, to ask the Court to be appointed to lead the case. The complaint asserts claims under Sections 10(b) and 20(a) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 on behalf of investors in CoreWeave securities. The case is pending in the U.S. District Court for the District of New Jersey and is captioned Masaitis v. CoreWeave, Inc., et al., No. 2:26-cv-00355.

Why is CoreWeave Being Sued For Securities Fraud?

CoreWeave is an AI-focused cloud computing company that builds and operates data centers offering high-performance GPU infrastructure. CoreWeave relies on multiple partners to develop its data centers and provide the infrastructure needed for its AI computing operations, including Core Scientific, a large digital infrastructure company. On July 7, 2025, CoreWeave announced a merger agreement with Core Scientific.

During the relevant period, CoreWeave repeatedly assured investors it could capitalize on the "robust" and "unprecedented" demand for its services given its "competitive strengths," including its ability to "deploy" AI infrastructure "at massive scale" and "rapidly scale our operations."

As alleged, in truth, CoreWeave overstated its ability to meet customer demand and concealed significant construction delays at its data centers.

Why did CoreWeave's Stock Drop?

On October 30, 2025, Core Scientific announced it did not receive enough shareholder votes to approve the merger with CoreWeave and, as a result, terminated the merger agreement. This news caused the price of CoreWeave stock to drop $8.87 per share, or more than 6%, from $139.93 per share on October 29, 2025, to $131.06 per share on October 30, 2025.

Then, on November 10, 2025, CoreWeave lowered guidance for revenue, operating income, capital spending, and active power capacity for 2025 due to "temporary delays related to a third-party data center developer who is behind schedule." This news caused the price of CoreWeave stock to drop $17.22 per share, or more than 16%, from $105.61 per share on November 10, 2025, to $88.39 per share on November 11, 2025.

Finally, on December 15, 2025, The Wall Street Journal reported that the "completion date" for a "huge data-center cluster" in Denton, Texas to be leased by OpenAI, "has been pushed back several months," and that the site builder, Core Scientific, had flagged delays at the site months earlier. The Wall Street Journal also reported that Core Scientific had flagged additional delays at sites in Texas and elsewhere "since at least February." This news caused the price of CoreWeave stock to drop $2.85 per share, or more than 3%, from $72.35 per share on December 15, 2025, to $69.50 per share on December 16, 2025.

Click here for more information: https://www.bfalaw.com/cases/coreweave-inc-class-action-lawsuit.

What Can You Do?

If you invested in CoreWeave, you may have legal options and are encouraged to submit your information to the firm.

All representation is on a contingency fee basis, there is no cost to you. Shareholders are not responsible for any court costs or expenses of litigation. The firm will seek court approval for any potential fees and expenses.

Submit your information by visiting:

https://www.bfalaw.com/cases/coreweave-inc-class-action-lawsuit

Or contact:

Adam McCall

adam@bfalaw.com

212.789.3619

Why Bleichmar Fonti & Auld LLP?

BFA is a leading international law firm representing plaintiffs in securities class actions and shareholder litigation. It has been named a top plaintiff law firm by Chambers USA, The Legal 500, and ISS SCAS, and its attorneys have been named "Elite Trial Lawyers" by the National Law Journal, "Litigation Stars" by Benchmark Litigation, among the top "500 Leading Plaintiff Financial Lawyers" by Lawdragon, "Titans of the Plaintiffs' Bar" by Law360 and "SuperLawyers" by Thomson Reuters. Among its recent notable successes, BFA recovered over $900 million in value from Tesla, Inc.'s Board of Directors, as well as $420 million from Teva Pharmaceutical Ind. Ltd.

For more information about BFA and its attorneys, please visit https://www.bfalaw.com.

https://www.bfalaw.com/cases/coreweave-inc-class-action-lawsuit

Attorney advertising. Past results do not guarantee future outcomes.

data.title: Teva Pharmaceuticals Seeks FDA Greenlight For New Schizophrenia Treatment
data.description:
U.S. affiliate of Teva Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd. (TEVA) said on Friday that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has accepted its New Drug Application (NDA) for olanzapine extended-release injectable suspension or TEV-'749 for the treatment of schizophrenia in adults.

The application was based on data from a late-stage trial in which TEV-'749 administered as a once-monthly subcutaneous injection demonstrated an efficacy and safety profile consistent with currently available olanzapine formulations and showed no evidence for the need for post-injection monitoring.

Shares of TEVA traded 1% lower at the time of writing. 

Get updates to this developing story directly on Stocktwits.

data.title: Wells Fargo Hires $3.1 Billion Advisory Team From J.P. Morgan — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Kenneth Corbin

Wells Fargo Advisors has recruited the Weikes Slattery Group from J.P. Morgan Securities, where the team managed $3.1 billion in client assets. The practice, led by Liz Weikes and John Slattery, joined Wells Fargo on Thursday.

Weikes says the move to join Wells Fargo "positions us to provide even greater flexibility, broader investment solutions, and enhanced support to meet the evolving needs of our clients." spokeswoman for J.P. Morgan declined to comment on the departure.

The Weikes Slattery Group is based in New York City and maintains a presence in San Diego and Palo Alto, Calif. The practice serves ultrahigh-net-worth individuals, family offices, and institutions. Wells Fargo says the group produced about $17 million in annual revenue with J.P. Morgan.

In addition to the practice's two namesake leaders, the Weikes Slattery Group includes Executive Director Joe Gillan, Vice Presidents Kevin Eisenberg and Bernadette Bucchere, and Clarke Eastman-Pinto, an associate.

Weikes entered the industry in 2009 as a broker with J.P. Morgan, registering as an investment advisor in 2009. She is dually registered with Wells Fargo.

Slattery is an industry veteran who broke into the sector with Merrill Lynch in 1994 and did stints at UBS, Citigroup, and Morgan Stanley before joining J.P. Morgan in 2018.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Traders Await Word on USMCA Exemption For Trump's Global 10% Tariff — Market Talk
data.description: Most exports from Canada and Mexico were exempt from the Ieepa tariffs that the Supreme Court struck down, so long as the shipments complied with USMCA terms. It's unclear whether a similar exemption, through USMCA compliance, will be available to two of America's biggest trading partners with President Trump's new 10% global tariff under Section 122 of the 1974 Trade Act, says Toronto trade and investment lawyer Mark Warner. Karl Schamotta, chief market strategist at forex firm Corpay, says the Trump administration likely has the authority to exempt certain countries, like Canada and Mexico, which have agreements like USMCA in place. "But our theory has not been legally tested," Schamotta says. (paul.vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

data.title: Ieepa Ruling Could Mute a Favorite Trump Diplomacy Tactic — Market Talk
data.description: President Trump says he will use other legal authorities to impose tariffs after the Supreme Court ruled illegal his use of Ieepa to impose levies. Other laws will let him replace some of the Ieepa duties, but many of those statutes--like the Trade Expansion Act of 1962 and the Trade Act of 1974--come with additional constraints, including rate ceilings, calendar limitations, and required investigations and paperwork. Over the past year, Trump has freely wielded tariffs as a weapon he can swing at his discretion: for instance, threatening tariffs for European countries that don't support his Greenland ambitions, or threatening to punish Canada with tariffs over a trade deal with China. With Ieepa removed from Trump's arsenal, such threats may likely fizzle. (matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattgrossman)

data.title: R/A: Solid loan and deposit growth, stable asset quality, and expanding digital business drive results
data.description:
Net income rose 7% YoY in 4Q25, with strong loan and deposit growth, though efficiency ratio increased and NIM declined. Asset quality remained robust, and digital platforms expanded, especially in business clients. Capitalization and governance standards were further strengthened.

data.title: Gold Showing Significant Move Back To The Upside
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Gold prices have moved sharply higher during trading on Friday, more than offsetting the modest pullback seen during the previous session.

After slipping $12.10 or 0.2 percent to $4,997.40 an ounce during Thursday's session, gold for April delivery is surging $98.20 or 2 percent to $5,095.60 an ounce.

The rebound by the price of gold comes as rising U.S.-Iran tensions have fueled safe-haven demand for the precious metal.

U.S.-Iran tensions intensified after U.S. President Donald Trump warned Tehran that it had a 'maximum' of 15 days to reach a nuclear deal with the U.S. or 'bad things will happen.'

With potential U.S. military action looming, Iran has warned that U.S. bases in the Middle East could be 'legitimate targets' if Washington attacks.

Traders are also reacting to news that the U.S. Supreme Court has struck down most of Trump's sweeping global tariffs, delivering a major blow to the president's signature economic policy.

The nation's highest court ruled in a 6-3 decision that the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, or IEEPA, does not authorize the president to impose tariffs.

However, the court's decision does not address whether the more than $130 billion in tariffs that has already been collected should be refunded, with Justice Brett Kavanaugh predicting 'that process is likely to be a 'mess.''

Trump slammed the 'deeply disappointing' decision in a post on Truth Social and announced plans to sign an executive order imposing a new 10 percent 'global tariff.'

On the U.S. economic front, the Commerce Department released a report showing U.S. economic growth slowed by much more than anticipated in the fourth quarter of 2025.

The report said gross domestic product climbed by 1.4 percent in the fourth quarter after surging by 4.4 percent in the third quarter. Economists had expected GDP to jump by 2.8 percent.

A separate Commerce Department report showed an unexpected uptick in the annual rate of consumer price growth, reinforcing expectations the Federal Reserve is likely to keep interest rates on hold in the near future.

data.title: U.S. Non-Housing Services Inflation Is a Line Item to Watch — Market Talk
data.description: At 3%, core PCE inflation remained firm in December. Cooler inflation trends may be ahead if goods prices and housing prices continue to decelerate. But core nonhousing services inflation--nicknamed "supercore" inflation--looks more stubborn, Kevin Burgett of Monetary Policy Analytics writes. He notes that supercore inflation was 3.7% annualized over the last 6 months of 2025, up from 3% in the first half of last year. "That second-half pace, if sustained, would be a problem," he writes. But he argues that cooling wage inflation offers reassurance that supercore may lose steam. (matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattgrossman)

data.title: How Does High-Altitude Living Lower Diabetes Risk?
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - For years, researchers have noticed that people living in high-altitude regions are often prone to diabetes. Based on this notion, researchers at the Gladstone Institutes explored the biological reason behind this.

During the research, Isha Jain's team studied hypoxia, a condition where oxygen levels in the blood drop, and how it affects the body. In earlier studies, they saw that mice exposed to low oxygen had much lower blood sugar levels. After eating, their bodies cleared sugar from the blood quickly, which is something that usually lowers the risk of diabetes.

'When we gave sugar to the mice in hypoxia, it disappeared from their bloodstream almost instantly,' said first author Yolanda Martí-Mateos, a postdoctoral scholar in Jain's lab. 'We looked at muscle, brain, liver-all the usual suspects-but nothing in these organs could explain what was happening.'

With a new imaging method, researchers discovered that red blood cells were actually taking in the extra sugar. More studies showed that in low oxygen conditions, mice produced more red blood cells. Not only that, each red blood cell absorbed more sugar than usual.

'Red blood cells represent a hidden compartment of glucose metabolism that has not been appreciated until now,' Jain said. 'This discovery could open up entirely new ways to think about controlling blood sugar.'

To understand how this works, Jain's team worked with experts Angelo D'Alessandro and Allan Doctor, who study red blood cells. They found that in low oxygen conditions, red blood cells use glucose to make a molecule that helps release more oxygen to body tissues, which is very useful when oxygen is scarce. Interestingly, the health benefits of low oxygen exposure lasted for weeks or even months after the mice returned to normal oxygen levels.

'This is just the beginning,' Jain added. 'There's still so much to learn about how the whole body adapts to changes in oxygen, and how we could leverage these mechanisms to treat a range of conditions.'

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: $41,608,200 Raised on Initial Closing. Books Now Re-Open for a Second Closing on April 1, 2026
data.description:
Maple Leaf Critical Minerals 2026 Enhanced Flow-Through Limited Partnership NATIONAL & QUEBEC CLASS

VANCOUVER, BC / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2026 / Maple Leaf Critical Minerals 2026 Enhanced Flow-Through Limited Partnership (the "Partnership") is pleased to announce that it has completed its initial closing on February 20, 2026 for gross proceeds of $41,608,200. The books are now reopened and will close on Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 12:00 p.m. (EST) for a second closing on Wednesday, April 1, 2026.

Partnership Objectives & Benefits - National Class Units

The Partnership is designed to provide Series A and Series F holders of National Class Units ("National Class Limited Partners") with an investment in a diversified portfolio of Flow-Through Shares of Resource Companies incurring Eligible Expenditures (as those terms are defined in the Prospectus) across Canada with a view to maximizing the tax benefits of an investment in National Class Units and achieving capital appreciation and/or income for National Class Limited Partners. National Class Limited Partners must be residents of Canada or liable to pay Canadian income tax. The Partnership intends to invest approximately 90% of the available funds in companies that are exploring for Critical Minerals to avail of the 30% Critical Minerals Tax Credit.

After accounting for tax credits, investors are expected to receive equivalent tax deductions of up to approximately 139%-147%

(depending on the marginal tax rate) of the amount invested based on and subject to certain conditions as set forth in the Prospectus.

Partnership Objectives & Benefits - Québec Class Units

The Partnership is designed to provide Series A and Series F holders of Québec Class Units ("Québec Class Limited Partners") with an investment in a diversified portfolio of Flow-Through Shares of Resource Companies incurring Eligible Expenditures principally in the Province of Québec with a view to maximizing the tax benefits of an investment in Québec Class Units and achieving capital appreciation and/or income for Québec Class Limited Partners. Québec Class Units are most suitable for investors who reside in the Province Québec or are liable to pay income tax in Québec. The Partnership intends to invest approximately 100% of the available funds in companies that are exploring for Critical Minerals to avail of the 30% Critical Minerals Tax Credit.

After accounting for tax credits, investors are expected to receive equivalent tax deductions of up to approximately 156% of the amount invested based on and subject to certain conditions as set forth in the Prospectus.

Liquidity Event

The investment portfolios of both the National and Québec Class Units will be actively managed in such a way as to preserve the ability to undertake a future liquidity event, such as a rollover into a mutual fund corporation.

The Syndicate

The syndicate of agents for the offering is being led by Scotia Capital Inc. and includes National Bank Financial Inc., BMO Nesbitt Burns Inc., CIBC World Markets Inc., iA Private Wealth Inc., Richardson Wealth Limited, Canaccord Genuity Corp., Desjardins Securities Inc., Manulife Wealth Inc., Raymond James Ltd., CI Investment Services Inc., Ventum Financial Cop. and Wellington-Altus Private Wealth. A copy of the Prospectus can be obtained from any agent.

Offering Jurisdictions

Each of the Provinces of Canada.

FOR FURTHER INFORMATION, PLEASE CONTACT

Hugh Cartwright, Chairman

MAPLE LEAF SHORT DURATION FLOW-THROUGH PROGRAMS

Tel: 1-866-688-5750

Email: info@mapleleaffunds.ca Web: www.MapleLeafFunds.ca

www.MapleLeafFunds.ca

A final prospectus dated January 27, 2026 relating to these securities has been filed with the securities commissions or similar authorities in each of the Provinces of Canada. This release shall not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of any offer to buy the securities. This release is provided for information purposes only. Commissions, trailing commissions, management fees and expenses all may be associated with investment funds. Please read the Prospectus before investing. Investment funds are not guaranteed, their values change frequently, and past performance may not be repeated. Capitalized terms not defined herein have the meanings set forth in the Prospectus.

SOURCE: Maple Leaf Critical Minerals 2026 Enhanced Flow-Through Limited Partnership

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: MINERALS TECHNOLOGIES INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Minerals Technologies Inc., a global leader in minerals-based technology solutions, has released its 2025 Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment. Despite a challenging year marked by declining demand and competitive pressures, the company remains focused on innovation, strategic growth, and maintaining financial stability.

Financial Highlights

Minerals Technologies Inc. reported the following key financial metrics for 2025:

  • Net Sales: $2,072.6 million, a 2% decrease from 2024, reflecting a challenging competitive environment and declining demand in certain markets.
  • Production Margin: $518.0 million, representing 25.0% of net sales, down from 25.9% in 2024.
  • Income from Operations: $47.4 million, a significant decrease from $286.5 million in 2024, primarily due to a $215 million provision for litigation accrual and credit losses.
  • Net Income (Loss) Attributable to Minerals Technologies Inc.: $(18.4) million, compared to $167.1 million in 2024, impacted by litigation and restructuring charges.
  • Diluted Earnings (Loss) Per Share: $(0.59), compared to $5.17 in 2024, reflecting the net loss and decreased income from operations.

Business Highlights

The company's business performance in 2025 included the following highlights:

  • Revenue Segments: The Consumer & Specialties segment accounted for 53% of net sales, with the Engineered Solutions segment contributing 47%. Within Consumer & Specialties, Household & Personal Care products generated $512.8 million, while Specialty Additives brought in $584.9 million. In the Engineered Solutions segment, High-Temperature Technologies accounted for $704.7 million, and Environmental & Infrastructure contributed $270.2 million.
  • Geographical Performance: Net sales in the United States decreased by 1% to $1.1 billion, representing 52% of consolidated net sales. International sales decreased by 3% to $1.0 billion, accounting for 48% of consolidated net sales.
  • Sales Units: The company is the world's largest producer of bentonite and a leading producer of calcium carbonate, with significant production volumes supporting its operations across various segments.
  • New Product Launches: The company focused on deploying new products in pet care, such as lightweight litter, and developing innovative applications for bleaching earth products in the edible oil and renewable fuel industries.
  • New Production Launches: The company operates satellite plants near paper or packaging mills, which eliminate transportation costs and support the production of customized Precipitated Calcium Carbonate (PCC) and Ground Calcium Carbonate (GCC) products.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to increase its presence in global cat litter products, expand its bentonite-based foundry solutions in emerging markets, and continue developing products for waste management and recycling to improve sustainability. Additionally, the company aims to expand its FLUORO-SORB® adsorbent products to address PFAS contamination globally.

Strategic Initiatives

Minerals Technologies Inc. is focusing on several strategic growth initiatives and capital management activities:

  • Strategic Growth Initiatives: The company is focusing on new product development, geographic expansion, and acquisitions. It aims to increase market share in global cat litter products, deploy new pet care products, and expand its presence in emerging markets. The company is also developing innovative applications for its products in various industries, such as personal care, fabric care, and environmental solutions.
  • Capital Management: The company has a strong balance sheet with over $700 million in available liquidity, including cash and credit facilities. It repurchased $58.5 million in shares under a $200 million buyback program and declared a quarterly dividend of $0.12 per share. The company refinanced its credit facilities, increasing its revolving credit facility to $400 million and its term loan facility to $575 million. It also issued $400 million in senior notes due 2028.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to maintain a balanced approach to capital deployment, focusing on investments in growth, shareholder returns, and debt reduction. It anticipates capital expenditures of $90 million to $100 million in 2026 to improve operations and meet strategic growth objectives. The company is also evaluating the impact of recent tariff changes and continues to monitor the economic effects of the trade environment.

Challenges and Risks

Minerals Technologies Inc. faces several challenges and risks, including:

  • Industry and Market Risks: The company faces significant industry and market risks due to global economic conditions, including declining consumer confidence, volatile raw material prices, and fluctuating interest and exchange rates. These factors could reduce demand for products and increase costs, impacting financial results. The cyclical nature of customer industries, such as paper and metalcasting, poses additional risks, as does competition from companies with greater resources. Consolidation in customer industries could also reduce sales and increase pressure on pricing.
  • Financial Risks: The need for significant cash to service debt could limit flexibility in responding to economic changes. The company's debt agreements contain covenants that could restrict operations, and failure to comply could lead to defaults.
  • Technology and Development Risks: Challenges in achieving growth initiatives, particularly in expanding into new geographic markets and consumer-oriented sectors, could adversely affect operations. Delays in new product development and competition in intellectual property could also impact the company.
  • Operational Risks: The bankruptcy filing of subsidiaries related to talc liabilities could have a material adverse effect on the company's financial condition. The company is also subject to stringent environmental, health, and safety regulations, which could lead to unanticipated costs or liabilities.
  • Management's Discussion and Analysis: The company reported a decrease in net sales and income from operations in 2025, primarily due to provisions for talc-related claims and restructuring charges. The global trade environment, including tariffs, remains uncertain and could impact demand and costs. Management is focused on innovation and new product development to drive growth, with strategic initiatives in expanding market presence and developing new applications for existing products. The company continues to monitor the economic effects of tariffs and is evaluating the impact of a recent Supreme Court ruling on tariffs. Management is also focused on maintaining a strong balance sheet and liquidity to support growth and shareholder returns.

SEC Filing: MINERALS TECHNOLOGIES INC [ MTX ] - 10-K - Feb. 20, 2026

data.title: LyondellBasell Industries N.V. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
LyondellBasell Industries N.V., a global leader in the chemical industry, has released its 2025 Form 10-K report, detailing the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's efforts to navigate a complex market environment while optimizing its portfolio and improving operational efficiency.

Financial Highlights

Sales and other operating revenues: $30,153 million, reflecting a decrease of $3,241 million or 10% compared to 2024, primarily due to lower average sales prices and volumes.

Cost of sales: $27,576 million, decreased by $1,174 million or 4% compared to 2024, mainly due to lower feedstock and energy costs.

Operating income (loss): $(420) million, a decrease of $2,338 million or 122% compared to 2024, driven by lower margins and impairment charges.

Net income (loss): $(738) million, compared to $1,367 million in 2024, impacted by impairment charges and lower operating income.

Earnings (loss) per share - Basic: $(2.34), compared to $4.16 in 2024, reflecting the decrease in net income.

Earnings (loss) per share - Diluted: $(2.34), compared to $4.15 in 2024, consistent with the basic EPS decline.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The Olefins and Polyolefins-Americas segment experienced a 15% decrease in revenues in 2025 compared to 2024, primarily due to lower average sales prices and volumes. The Olefins and Polyolefins-Europe, Asia, International segment saw a 6% decrease in revenues, driven by lower sales prices and volumes, partially offset by favorable foreign exchange impacts.

Geographical Performance: In Europe, the petrochemical industry faced significant challenges, with a prolonged downturn affecting the Olefins and Polyolefins-Europe, Asia, International segment. The region's performance was impacted by increased import competition and a decrease in naphtha prices.

Sales Units: The Olefins and Polyolefins-Americas segment reported lower volumes due to planned and unplanned outages, contributing to a 6% decrease in revenue. The Intermediates and Derivatives segment experienced a decline in sales volumes following the sale of the EO&D business.

New Production Launches: In 2025, the company permanently closed its European PO Joint Venture, incurring shutdown costs. This decision was part of a broader strategy to optimize the portfolio and improve operational efficiency.

Future Outlook: For the first quarter of 2026, the company plans to align operating rates with global demand, targeting approximately 85% for O&P-Americas, 75% for O&P-EAI, and 85% for I&D assets. The company anticipates improved demand in O&P-EAI due to typical seasonal trends and expects oxyfuel profitability to normalize in the I&D segment.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: In 2025, LyondellBasell Industries N.V. announced a cash improvement plan targeting $600 million in annualized savings, which was exceeded with $800 million achieved. The plan included deferral of capital spending, net reduction in accounts receivable, inventory, and accounts payable, and fixed cost reductions. The company also agreed to sell certain European olefins and polyolefins assets, expecting to recognize a loss of $700 million to $900 million upon closing in 2026.

Capital Management: The company returned $2.0 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases in 2025. It issued $2.5 billion in new debt through guaranteed notes due in 2031, 2035, and 2036, while repaying $492 million of outstanding principal of 1.25% guaranteed notes due 2025. The company maintained a strong liquidity position with $3,443 million in cash and cash equivalents at the end of 2025. Additionally, LyondellBasell repurchased approximately 3.0 million shares for $201 million under its share repurchase authorization.

Future Outlook: For 2026, LyondellBasell plans to invest approximately $1.2 billion in capital expenditures, with $800 million allocated for sustaining maintenance and the remainder for growth projects. The company aims to generate an additional $500 million in cash savings, targeting a cumulative $1.3 billion in savings. It intends to continue providing shareholder returns through dividends and share repurchases, while maintaining an investment-grade balance sheet. The company declared a reduced quarterly dividend of $0.69 per share in February 2026, reflecting a strategic adjustment in capital allocation.

Challenges and Risks

Challenges and Risks: The company faces significant risks due to the cyclicality and volatility of the chemical industry, which can lead to fluctuations in operating results. New capacity additions globally have led to periods of oversupply, impacting profitability. A sustained decrease in crude oil prices may adversely affect operations, particularly in North America. The company also faces challenges with the costs and supply limitations of raw materials and energy, which could increase operating expenses and reduce liquidity. Political instability in regions supplying raw materials poses additional risks, potentially affecting supply and pricing.

The business is capital intensive, relying on cash from operations and external financing. Limitations on access to financing could adversely affect operations, dividends, and growth. The company is also exposed to risks inherent in the chemical industry, including potential liabilities from natural disasters, cybersecurity incidents, and other hazards. Interruptions at facilities due to these risks could lead to increased liabilities or lower operating results.

In 2025, the company faced significant headwinds from global trade disruptions, falling oil prices, and capacity additions outpacing demand growth. Non-cash impairment charges were recognized due to a prolonged downturn in the European petrochemical and global automotive industries. The company is implementing a cash improvement plan to address macroeconomic volatility, targeting $1.3 billion in cumulative cash savings by 2026.

The company agreed to sell certain European assets, expecting a loss of $700 million to $900 million upon closing. This sale reflects strategic adjustments in response to market conditions. The company also faces challenges from lower margins in its O&P-Americas segment due to trade issues and higher feedstock costs.

The company is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in currency exchange rates, which may affect financial results. The evolving regulatory environment, particularly regarding climate change and emissions, could lead to increased compliance costs and operational restrictions. The company is also addressing risks related to cybersecurity threats, which could impact data integrity and operations.

Overall, the company is navigating a complex landscape of market, operational, and regulatory risks, with strategic initiatives in place to mitigate these challenges and adapt to changing conditions.

SEC Filing: LyondellBasell Industries N.V. [ LYB ] - 10-K - Feb. 20, 2026

data.title: AMKOR TECHNOLOGY, INC. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Amkor Technology, Inc., a leading provider of semiconductor packaging and test services, has released its 2025 Form 10-K report. The report details the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the volatile semiconductor industry. This comprehensive summary provides insights into Amkor's achievements and future plans as it continues to navigate a dynamic market environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $6,707.981 million, increased by 6.2% from 2024 primarily due to higher sales across all end markets.
  • Gross Profit: $938.599 million, with a gross margin of 14.0%, decreased from 14.8% in 2024 due to increased overhead and employee compensation costs.
  • Operating Income: $467.385 million, with an operating income margin of 7.0%, slightly up from 6.9% in 2024, primarily due to the net amount recognized for a cash receipt subject to bankruptcy proceedings.
  • Net Income: $373.895 million, consistent with the previous year, reflecting stable performance despite increased costs.
  • Net Income Attributable to Amkor Per Common Share - Basic: $1.51, showing a slight increase from $1.44 in 2024.
  • Net Income Attributable to Amkor Per Common Share - Diluted: $1.50, up from $1.43 in 2024, indicating improved earnings per share.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Amkor's revenue is divided into Advanced Products and Mainstream Products. Advanced Products, which include flip-chip, memory, and wafer-level packages, accounted for 82.8% of net sales in 2025. Mainstream Products, which use wirebond interconnect technology, accounted for 17.2% of net sales.
  • Geographical Performance: Amkor's manufacturing footprint spans key regions in Asia and Europe, with a new facility under construction in Arizona, USA, to enhance participation in U.S. semiconductor initiatives. The Vietnam facility, which opened in 2024, is scaling production to increase capacity and operational flexibility in Asia.
  • New Production Launches: Construction of a new facility in Arizona began in the second half of 2025, aimed at strengthening Amkor's ability to serve customers seeking to regionalize their supply chains. The Vietnam facility, opened in 2024, is in the early stages of high-volume manufacturing.
  • Sales Units: The company provides a comprehensive range of packaging and test services, including wafer bump, wafer probe, wafer back-grind, packaging, burn-in, system level and final test, and drop shipment services. These services are used by integrated device manufacturers, fabless semiconductor companies, OEMs, and contract foundries.
  • New Product Launches: Amkor collaborates with leading semiconductor companies to co-develop innovative solutions such as wafer-level fan-out, Silicon Photonics, Co-Packaged Optics, and high-power modules incorporating gallium nitride and silicon carbide devices.
  • Future Outlook: Amkor plans to continue investing in advanced packaging and test technologies, expanding its global manufacturing footprint, and deepening strategic partnerships with leading semiconductor companies. The company expects its 2026 capital expenditures to be approximately $2.5 billion to $3.0 billion, primarily due to the construction of the Arizona Facility.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: Amkor Technology, Inc. is expanding its manufacturing footprint with the construction of a new facility in Arizona, which began in the second half of 2025. This strategic investment aims to strengthen the company's ability to serve customers seeking to regionalize their supply chains and enhance participation in U.S. semiconductor initiatives. Additionally, Amkor is scaling production in its Vietnam facility, which opened in 2024, to increase capacity and operational flexibility in Asia. The company is also focusing on advanced packaging and test technologies to support key markets such as HPC, AI, automotive, IoT, and mobile communications.
  • Capital Management: In 2025, Amkor's capital expenditures totaled $904.6 million, primarily focused on investments in advanced packaging and test equipment and the Arizona Facility. The company entered into a $1.0 billion senior secured revolving credit facility in May 2025, which includes an uncommitted optional accordion of up to $200 million. In September 2025, Amkor issued $500 million of 5.875% Senior Notes due October 2033, using the proceeds for the redemption of outstanding 2027 Notes and general corporate purposes. The company also paid total cash dividends of $81.9 million in 2025 and announced a 1% increase in its quarterly dividend rate.
  • Future Outlook: Amkor expects its 2026 capital expenditures to be approximately $2.5 billion to $3.0 billion, primarily due to the construction of the Arizona Facility. The company plans to continue making prudent investments and closely managing capacity expansion and cost control in response to market conditions. Amkor is also eligible to receive an investment tax credit on qualified investments in U.S. semiconductor manufacturing under the CHIPS Act, with the rate increased to 35% for qualified property placed in service after 2025. The company intends to return 40% to 50% of cumulative free cash flow over time through dividends and stock repurchases, subject to strategic investments and other capital allocation priorities.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Risks: The semiconductor industry is cyclical, and downturns can lead to reduced demand, excess capacity, and lower sales. The company is also vulnerable to fluctuations in raw material costs and supply chain disruptions, which can impact its operations and financial performance.
  • Operational Risks: The company faces competition from established competitors and internal capabilities of IDMs, as well as from foundries and contract manufacturers. Amkor's ability to maintain high-capacity utilization rates is critical due to its high fixed costs. The lack of contractually committed customer demand adds to the uncertainty in forecasting future sales.
  • Regulatory Risks: Amkor's international operations expose it to risks related to trade restrictions, regional conflicts, and compliance with environmental and safety regulations. The company must also navigate complex tax laws and potential changes in tax regulations that could impact its financial condition.
  • Cybersecurity and Intellectual Property Risks: The company must protect its proprietary technology and operate without infringing on others' rights. Cybersecurity threats pose a risk to its operations and reputation.
  • Indebtedness Risks: Amkor's substantial debt could limit its financial flexibility and increase its vulnerability to economic downturns. Rising interest rates could further impact its financial condition.
  • Human Capital Risks: Attracting and retaining qualified personnel is crucial for Amkor's operations, and labor shortages could increase costs.
  • Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by the cyclical nature of the semiconductor industry and the need for strategic investments in technology and capacity to remain competitive. The company is focused on expanding its geographic footprint and enhancing strategic partnerships to mitigate risks and capture growth opportunities in high-value markets such as automotive, IoT, and mobile communications.
  • Market Risk Management: Amkor is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in interest rates and foreign currency exchange rates, which could adversely affect its financial results. The company actively monitors these risks and employs strategies to mitigate their impact, such as using financial instruments to hedge against currency and interest rate fluctuations.

SEC Filing: AMKOR TECHNOLOGY, INC. [ AMKR ] - 10-K - Feb. 20, 2026
